explicit_text: I grew up in Tulsa, Oklahoma, where I attended my first school, Longfellow. After a brief stay in Portland, Oregon, we returned to Tulsa and I continued my education there at Whittier School, Grover Cleveland Junior High, and finally Will Rogers High School, graduating in 1953. Tulsa was home for most of my childhood and teenage years, and many of my early memories, including living near Archer Park and visiting downtown stores, are rooted there.
implicit_text: I grew up in a city in northeastern Oklahoma, where I attended my first school. After a brief stay in a Pacific Northwest city, we returned to that Oklahoma city and I continued my education there at several local schools, graduating high school in 1953. That city was home for most of my childhood and teenage years, and many of my early memories, including living near a local park and visiting downtown stores, are rooted there.

explicit_text: The first school I attended was Longfellow School in Tulsa, Oklahoma. It was located at 6th and Peoria, but the original building is gone now and replaced by a new one. I was there during my Pre-Prime Harry and Kindergarten years before we moved briefly to Portland, Oregon.
implicit_text: The first school I attended was an elementary school located at 6th and Peoria in that Oklahoma city. The original building is gone now and replaced by a new one. I was there during my earliest school years before we moved briefly to the Pacific Northwest.

explicit_text: My family and I lived in Portland, Oregon for about a year during the early 1940s. My dad had volunteered for the army but was turned down because of polio, so he found work in the shipyards along the Willamette River. We stayed there until he secured a house back in Tulsa, after which we returned home.

explicit_text: After returning from Portland, I was enrolled at Whittier School in Tulsa, where I attended from third through sixth grade. We were living with an aunt near Zunis and 3rd Street at the time. Whittier was an important part of my early education before I moved on to junior high.

explicit_text: Following my years at Whittier School, I attended Grover Cleveland Junior High School in Tulsa. This was the next step in my education before I went on to high school at Will Rogers. It was part of my schooling during the late 1940s.

explicit_text: I graduated from Will Rogers High School in Tulsa in 1953. It was the final stage of my formal education before law school. Attending Will Rogers was a significant part of my youth, and it followed my time at Grover Cleveland Junior High.

explicit_text: Archer Park was right across the street from the house we bought in 1944 at 2854 East Archer. The park was central to much of our entertainment as kids, with swings and organized games during the summer. The city hired men and women to supervise and keep us busy, and I remember a man named John who was very active in organizing activities there.

explicit_text: The Circle Theatre was a key place for entertainment in my childhood. I loved going there to watch movies, especially Hopalong Cassidy, who was my hero. The theater was part of a small building that also housed Doc's Candy Store, and sometimes our parents would take us to see films there.

explicit_text: Doc's Candy Store was part of the same building as the Circle Theatre in Tulsa. After watching movies like Hopalong Cassidy, we would often go into Doc's to get candy. I think the building might still be there, and it was a memorable part of our entertainment routine.

explicit_text: Mr. Griffin's Hamburger was a small hamburger stand located at Braden and Anvil in Tulsa. It was a narrow building with a counter and stools, no air conditioning, just a ventilator fan. Dad would sometimes take us there for hamburgers and Pepsi, and the smell of onions cooking on the grill was unforgettable—it was a real treat for us back then.

explicit_text: I had an uncle who joined the United States Marine Corps around the time of World War II. While I was too young to remember the announcement of the war, I recall that my uncle served in the Marines during that period. His service was part of the larger context of the war impacting our family.

explicit_text: I was about seven years old when the attack on Pearl Harbor happened on December 7, 1941. I don't remember the announcement itself, but I do remember the events that followed and how it affected my family, including my uncle who joined the Marine Corps. That event marked the beginning of a significant time in our lives.
